name: azure-mgmt-weightsandbiases-dotnet
description: Azure Weights & Biases SDK for .NET. Manage W&B ML experiment tracking instances via Azure Marketplace.
capabilities: Create and provision Weights & Biases instances from Azure Marketplace
Configure Entra ID single sign-on for W&B workspaces
List and manage W&B instances across subscriptions and resource groups
Apply managed identity for secure Azure resource access
Update instance tags and configuration settings
Delete W&B instances and clean up resources
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines, identifying no security patterns. Manual review confirms this is a documentation-only skill providing guidance for Azure.ResourceManager.WeightsAndBiases .NET SDK. No executable code, network calls, or credential handling detected. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Machine learning teams need to deploy and manage Weights & Biases experiment tracking infrastructure on Azure. This skill provides .NET SDK guidance for provisioning W&B instances, configuring SSO, and managing ML observability resources.
userTitle: Manage W&B ML Experiments on Azure with .NET
limitations: Requires Azure subscription with Marketplace purchase permissions
SDK is in beta preview (v1.0.0-beta.1) with API version 2024-09-18-preview
Does not interact directly with W&B experiment data - only manages Azure infrastructure
Requires DefaultAzureCredential or equivalent Azure authentication setup

useCases.title: ML Platform Engineer
useCases.description: Automate W&B instance provisioning across development, staging, and production environments using Infrastructure as Code patterns.

useCases.title: Enterprise Data Science Team
useCases.description: Deploy W&B with enterprise SSO integration for centralized ML experiment tracking across the organization.

useCases.title: Cloud Cost Optimization
useCases.description: Programmatically manage W&B instance lifecycle to control costs by scaling resources based on project needs.

promptTemplates.title: Create a W&B Instance
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Weights & Biases instance in my Azure resource group with default configuration for a small ML team.

promptTemplates.title: Configure SSO for W&B
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up Entra ID single sign-on for my existing W&B instance using SAML authentication with allowed domains.

promptTemplates.title: List W&B Resources
promptTemplates.prompt: List all Weights & Biases instances in my subscription and show their provisioning state and region.

promptTemplates.title: Production W&B Deployment
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a production-ready W&B instance with managed identity enabled, SSO configured, proper tagging for cost tracking, and error handling for deployment failures.

bestPractices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for flexible authentication that works in development and production environments.
Enable managed identity on W&B instances to securely access other Azure resources without storing credentials.
Wait for provisioning state to reach Succeeded before using the instance for ML workloads.
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode subscription IDs or resource names - use environment variables or configuration files.
Avoid creating W&B instances without tags - this makes cost allocation and resource organization difficult.
Do not skip SSO configuration for enterprise deployments - manual user management creates security risks.

faq.question: What Azure regions support Weights & Biases instances?
faq.answer: Supported regions include East US, Central US, West US, West Europe, Japan East, and Korea Central. Choose the region closest to your compute resources for optimal latency.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure for W&B management?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which supports multiple authentication methods including Azure CLI, managed identity, Visual Studio credentials, and environment variables.

faq.question: Can I integrate W&B with Azure Active Directory for SSO?
faq.answer: Yes, configure SingleSignOnPropertiesV2 with SAML type, provide your Entra enterprise app ID, and specify allowed domains for automatic user provisioning.

faq.question: What is the difference between this SDK and the W&B Python SDK?
faq.answer: This .NET SDK manages Azure infrastructure for W&B instances. The W&B Python SDK is used by data scientists to log experiments, metrics, and models to an existing W&B workspace.

faq.question: How do I check if a W&B instance name is available?
faq.answer: Attempt to retrieve the instance by name. A 404 response indicates the name is available. A successful response means the name is already taken.

faq.question: Is the Azure.ResourceManager.WeightsAndBiases SDK production ready?
faq.answer: The SDK is currently in beta preview (v1.0.0-beta.1). It is functional but APIs may change. Test thoroughly before using in critical production workflows.
